summary: The reproduction bundle builds n8n from source, starts the server, creates an authenticated user with workflow:create scope, starts a mock internal HTTP server, and calls the vulnerable GET /rest/workflows/from-url endpoint targeting the internal service to demonstrate SSRF.
expected_security_impact: The n8n server should fetch the mock internal service URL via the from-url endpoint and return the response body to the attacker, demonstrating that an authenticated user can use the n8n server as an SSRF proxy to reach internal network resources.
actual_observed_result: PoC was not executed in this session. The reproduction bundle was generated based on code analysis confirming the vulnerable path exists.
verification_method: The verify.sh script checks that the response from the from-url endpoint contains data served by the mock internal HTTP service, confirming the n8n server fetched the internal URL and returned the contents.
limitations_or_blockers: PoC was not executed due to time and resource constraints. Building n8n from source requires Node.js 22+, pnpm 10+, and significant build time. The vulnerability was confirmed through static code analysis.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned version tag, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
